what_it_does: Defines a single static HTML/CSS hero composition for Studio V on a fixed 1600 by 900 canvas, using warm layered light, brand text, headline, an information card, hotspot markers, a menu pill, a circular V mark, and a bottom hatch strip.
observations: The document is self-contained except for a Google Fonts stylesheet for Plus Jakarta Sans.
The CSS uses root color variables for gold, bronze, charcoal, body text, and line color.
The main .view is a fixed-size artboard centered in the browser body.
The background is built from layered radial and linear gradients to simulate warm diffuse light.
A pseudo-element adds soft diagonal window-light bands with screen blending and masking.
A low-opacity grid-like .plan element sits in the lower-left as an architectural drawing cue.
The visible content includes Studio V branding, an atelier eyebrow, a large headline, an info callout, two hotspot dots, a menu pill, a V mark, and a gold hatch footer.
Most elements are absolutely positioned inside the artboard.
standouts: Warm light is represented through multiple diffuse gradient layers rather than a single flat gradient.
The composition combines editorial hero typography with spatial design cues such as hotspots, plan-grid texture, hatch marks, and an information callout.
The file presents the visual as a static scene rather than an interactive page.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for shared palette values.
Keeps markup minimal and relies heavily on class-based CSS for visual construction.
Uses absolute positioning for hero composition control.
Uses semantic HTML only lightly; most visual elements are generic div/span structures.
Includes an inline comment explaining the light treatment as a stand-in for a real render.
concerns: The 1600 by 900 fixed canvas does not include responsive rules.
The menu and hotspot markers look interactive but have no behavior or accessibility semantics.
The external font dependency can affect rendering if the font request fails.
The design relies on visual-only elements without ARIA labels or alternative descriptions.
The centered fixed artboard may overflow or scale poorly on smaller viewports.
notes: A static Studio V soft-light hero mockup that explores warm spatial atmosphere, brand presence, and architectural annotation motifs.
questions: Are the hotspot dots meant to become interactive annotations?
Is the fixed 1600 by 900 canvas intended as a screenshot artboard rather than a responsive page?
Is the gradient-based lighting only a placeholder for a rendered background image?
Should the menu pill perform navigation in a later version?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated title, "Var 3 — soft-light hero", matches the file: it implements a soft-light hero variation as a static HTML/CSS composition.
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to test a calm, warm, spatially informed hero direction for Studio V, using light, restrained typography, and architectural markers to suggest composed design work.
novelty: The distinct element is the combination of soft pooled lighting, masked window-light bands, faint plan-grid texture, hotspot markers, and hatch detailing inside a fixed hero artboard.
contribution: Contributes a warm atmospheric hero substrate with Studio V branding, diffuse light construction, architectural plan texture, annotation-style hotspots, and compact UI marks that a fused system could preserve as a visual language option.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS Studio V soft-light hero artboard with warm layered gradients, large editorial headline, brand mark, info card, hotspots, menu pill, plan texture, and hatch footer.
intention_for_embedding: Created to explore a calm atelier-style hero composition where warm light and architectural cues communicate composed design complexity.
